metadata.name: kyvern_policy
metadata.description: Kyvern on-chain policy program — extends Squads v4 spending limits with merchant allowlist, velocity cap, memo enforcement, and pause for AI-agent payments on Solana.

instructions.name: execute_payment
docs: Execute a payment through this vault.
Order of validation:
1. Vault not paused
2. Amount is positive and within the per-tx cap
3. Merchant hash is on the allowlist (or allowlist is empty)
4. Memo is present if `require_memo`
5. Velocity cap not exceeded (sliding window)
If all checks pass, this ix CPIs into Squads v4's
`spending_limit_use` with the amount + memo. Squads then enforces
its own daily/weekly cap. Failure at either layer reverts the
whole tx — no middle state, no off-chain trust.

accounts.name: policy
docs: Our policy PDA. Mutated to update the velocity counter.

accounts.name: member
docs: The agent delegate — must be a `member` on the referenced Squads
spending limit. Forwards signer privilege to the inner CPI.

accounts.name: multisig
docs: Squads v4 multisig PDA.

accounts.name: spending_limit
docs: Squads spending-limit PDA for this agent/member.

accounts.name: mint
docs: SPL mint for the payment (USDC on devnet/mainnet).

accounts.name: vault
docs: Squads vault PDA (the SOL-funded treasury wrapper).

accounts.name: vault_token_account
docs: Vault's USDC ATA (source of funds).

accounts.name: destination
docs: Recipient wallet (owner of destination_token_account).

accounts.name: destination_token_account
docs: Recipient's USDC ATA.

accounts.name: squads_program
docs: The Squads v4 program itself (target of the CPI). Address-checked
so this program can never be tricked into calling a clone.

accounts.name: token_program
docs: SPL Token program. Passed through to Squads CPI.

instructions.name: initialize_policy
docs: Initializes a new policy PDA for a given Squads multisig.
One multisig = one policy. The `authority` is the owner wallet
authorized to update the policy or pause it. The `member` that can
invoke `execute_payment` is derived from the attached Squads
spending-limit PDA — this program does not maintain a separate
member list.

accounts.name: multisig
docs: The Squads multisig PDA this policy governs. Passed as
`UncheckedAccount` — we *don't* validate its program owner here
because we only need the pubkey to derive our own PDA. The
execute_payment ix later enforces that this account is actually
owned by the Squads program via CPI.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Owner / admin wallet for this policy. Must sign; fee payer.

accounts.name: policy
docs: The PolicyAccount PDA for this multisig.

instructions.name: pause
docs: Pause the vault — every subsequent `execute_payment` will fail with
`KyvernError::VaultPaused` before it ever touches Squads.

instructions.name: resume
docs: Resume a paused vault.

instructions.name: swap_via_oracle
docs: Phase 1 (KYVERN_FRONTIER_GRAND_CHAMPION) — chain-enforced swap.
Fires a USDC → target-token swap from the vault, priced against
an off-chain oracle that signs (target_mint, price, expo, slot).
The program enforces:
1. Vault not paused
2. amount_in > 0 AND ≤ MAX_SWAP_USDC_BASE_UNITS AND ≤ per-tx cap
3. Oracle signer matches ORACLE_SIGNER
4. Oracle slot age within `max_oracle_age_slots` (staleness)
5. Slippage: amount_out ≥ min_amount_out (after 1% Kyvern fee)
6. Velocity cap (sliding window — same path as execute_payment)
Settlement happens in two transfers (caller composes them as a
single atomic tx — the program emits the receipt after both):
a. Vault USDC → treasury USDC sink (CPI to Squads spending_limit_use)
b. Treasury target-token PDA → user wallet ATA (signed by treasury PDA)
For Phase 1 ship: the program only validates + emits + advances
the velocity counter. The two transfers are composed by the
runtime side. Future-hardening: fold the second transfer into
this instruction so the whole swap is atomic.

accounts.name: policy
docs: Our policy PDA — same one execute_payment uses.

accounts.name: member
docs: The agent delegate — must be a `member` on the Squads spending
limit. Same trust model as execute_payment.

accounts.name: oracle_signer
docs: The oracle keypair signing this price. Must match ORACLE_SIGNER
(set at deploy time to the team's signer pubkey).

instructions.name: update_allowlist
docs: Replace the merchant allowlist. Only the policy authority may call.
Passing an empty `Vec` is a *valid* update that means "merchant
allowlist disabled" — every merchant hash will be accepted. (This
mirrors the `allowedMerchants: []` semantics in the off-chain
policy engine.)

errors.name: VaultPaused
errors.msg: Vault is paused by the owner (kill switch)

errors.name: InvalidAmount
errors.msg: Payment amount must be strictly positive

errors.name: AmountExceedsPerTxMax
errors.msg: Amount exceeds the per-transaction USDC cap for this vault

errors.name: MerchantNotAllowlisted
errors.msg: Merchant hash is not on this vault's allowlist

errors.name: MissingMemo
errors.msg: A non-empty memo is required for this vault

errors.name: VelocityCapExceeded
errors.msg: Velocity cap exceeded for the current window

errors.name: MemoTooLong
errors.msg: Memo exceeds the protocol-level maximum length

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Only the policy authority may perform this action

errors.name: NotASquadsMultisig
errors.msg: Supplied multisig account is not owned by the Squads v4 program

errors.name: SquadsCpiRejected
errors.msg: Squads CPI rejected the spending_limit_use invocation

errors.name: UntrustedOracle
errors.msg: Oracle signer does not match the trusted ORACLE_SIGNER pubkey

errors.name: OraclePriceStale
errors.msg: Oracle price is older than max_oracle_age_slots

errors.name: SlippageExceeded
errors.msg: Slippage exceeded — output amount below min_amount_out

errors.name: SwapAmountTooLarge
errors.msg: Swap input exceeds the program-level MAX_SWAP_USDC_BASE_UNITS ceiling

errors.name: SwapMathOverflow
errors.msg: Swap math overflow — adjust decimals / price exponent

fields.name: amount
docs: USDC base units to transfer. 6 decimals → $0.50 = 500_000.

fields.name: decimals
docs: Decimals of the mint (6 for USDC). Kept explicit because Squads
requires it in the inner `spending_limit_use` args.

fields.name: merchant_hash
docs: SHA-256 of the normalized merchant hostname (pre-computed
client-side; keeps the program scan cheap and locale-agnostic).

fields.name: memo
docs: Optional memo. Required when `policy.require_memo` is set.

fields.name: per_tx_max_base_units
docs: Per-transaction USDC cap in base units (USDC has 6 decimals, so

fields.name: merchant_allowlist
docs: SHA-256 hashes of allowed merchants, pre-computed client-side.
Empty Vec = any merchant (disabled allowlist).

fields.name: authority
docs: Who may update / pause this policy.

fields.name: multisig
docs: The Squads multisig this policy protects.

fields.name: per_tx_max_base_units
docs: Max USDC per single call (base units).

fields.name: paused
docs: Kill switch.

fields.name: require_memo
docs: When true, every call must include a non-empty memo.

fields.name: velocity_window_seconds
docs: Sliding window size (seconds).

fields.name: velocity_max_calls
docs: Max allowed calls per window.

fields.name: velocity_window_start
docs: Unix timestamp of the current window's start.

fields.name: velocity_calls_in_window
docs: Calls accumulated inside the current window.

fields.name: merchant_allowlist
docs: SHA-256(hostname) for every allowed merchant. Linear scan on
execute_payment — bounded by MAX_ALLOWLIST_SIZE.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump seed (seeds = [b"kyvern-policy-v1", multisig.key()]).

fields.name: amount_in
docs: USDC base units in (6 decimals).

fields.name: min_amount_out
docs: Slippage protection — minimum acceptable target-token output.

fields.name: target_token_mint
docs: Target token mint (e.g. wrapped SOL, kBONK, kJUP).

fields.name: target_decimals
docs: Target token decimals — needed for output math.

fields.name: oracle_price
docs: Oracle-published price (Pyth-style: real_price = price * 10^expo).

fields.name: oracle_expo
docs: Pyth-style exponent (typically negative).

fields.name: oracle_published_slot
docs: Slot the oracle price was published at — staleness check.

fields.name: max_oracle_age_slots
docs: Maximum acceptable slot age (60 ≈ 24s on devnet).
